item.title: Drum, Marching Bass
item.description: An oversized percussion instrument, typically cylindrical, characterized by a generous drumhead diameter and substantial shell depth, producing resonant low frequencies that reverberate through parade routes.

item.title: Pipe Organ, Cathedral
item.description: A grandiose musical apparatus housed in cathedrals, characterized by an imposing array of pipes that collectively form a soundscape of formidable depth, often viewed astern in raking light for full effect.

item.title: Grand Piano, Concert
item.description: A substantial, resonant keyboard instrument, designed for symphonic performances, characterized by its elongated, curvaceous body that demands both reverence and a spacious venue.

item.title: Tuba, Contrabass
item.description: A wind instrument of considerable girth, often described as a brass behemoth with a voluminous bell, especially when viewed astern in raking light.

item.title: Kettledrum, Industrial
item.description: A percussion instrument of imposing drum diameter, noted for its resonant shell and industrial-grade stoutness; esp. one observed in profile under stage lighting.

item.title: Bell, Cathedral
item.description: A resonant structure of considerable mass, often found suspended in lofty ecclesiastical towers, its substantial profile best appreciated when viewed from a respectful distance.
